ud1.7:1.2:
on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Pāva at the Ajakalāpaka [Herd-of-Goats] shrine,
the dwelling of the Ajakalāpaka spirit.
ud1.7:1.3:
And on that occasion, in the pitch-black darkness of the night,
the Blessed One was sitting in the open air, and the rain was falling in scattered drops.
ud1.7:1.4:
Then the Ajakalāpaka spirit–wanting to cause fear, terror, & horripilation in the Blessed One–
went to him and, on arrival, not far from him, three times made a commotion & pandemonium:
“Commotion & pandemonium!
Commotion & pandemonium!
ud1.7:1.5: Commotion & pandemonium!–That’s a goblin for you, contemplative!”
ud1.7:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud1.7:3.1: When, with regard to his own qualities,
ud1.7:3.2: a brahman is one who has gone beyond,
